content: Uncertainty scores from active agent memory selection can act as a dynamic relevance weight that modulates retrieval ranking in retrieval-augmented agent architectures, creating a self-improving memory loop where the agent prioritizes retrieving high-uncertainty samples that will most reduce its prediction error over time. This replaces static similarity-based ranking with an error-reduction oriented ranking aligned with the agent's ongoing learning objectives.
source_topics: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
trigger_topic: Uncertainty-based memory sample selection for deep active agents
